skill.name: dependency-management
skill.description: Dependency management specialist for auditing packages, reviewing updates, checking vulnerabilities, and assessing license compliance across common package managers.
skill.summary: Audit dependencies, plan safe updates, and review security or license risks across common package managers.
skill.author: Joseph OBrien

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command, weak cryptography, and sensitive-file patterns. Review found these are Markdown command examples, dependency audit placeholders, or field-name false positives, with no evidence of malicious intent, exfiltration, or prompt injection. The skill is low risk because its guidance includes package manager commands that users should review before running.

low_findings.title: Package Manager Command Examples Are Documentation
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged many fenced command examples as shell execution. These are Markdown instructions for dependency auditing and updates, not code that runs automatically. Some commands can modify dependency files when a user chooses to run them, so they should be reviewed before execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged items are inside Markdown code fences or template placeholders. No file contains code that invokes these commands automatically.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Placeholder False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography patterns in dependency descriptions and CVE table placeholders. The reviewed lines do not implement hashing, encryption, or authentication logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is descriptive text or audit report placeholders. There is no cryptographic API, algorithm selection, or security-sensitive implementation.

low_findings.title: Sensitive File Alert Is A Field Name False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged a certificate or key pattern in an npm license-report command. The term key appears as a dependency object field in a jq expression, not as a secret, certificate, or private key file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line transforms dependency object keys into package names. It does not reference credential files or expose secret material.

content.value_statement: Dependency drift creates security, compatibility, and license risks in active projects. This skill guides structured audits, safe update plans, and compliance reviews.
actual_capabilities: Identifies common dependency audit tasks for npm, pip, Maven, Gradle, Poetry, and Cargo.
Provides package manager commands for listing, updating, and auditing dependencies.
Guides vulnerability review with severity, affected package, and fix version details.
Supports license compliance reporting and incompatible license review.
Includes a helper script for parsing dependency files into structured dependency data.
Provides a dependency audit report template for security, update, and license findings.
limitations: It does not replace official vulnerability databases or package manager audit tools.
Some update commands can change lock files or dependency versions when run.
The helper parser supports basic formats and may miss complex pyproject dependency declarations.
License decisions still require project policy and legal review.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Security Update Plan
use_cases.description: Review vulnerable packages, rank fixes by severity, and plan low-risk update steps.

use_cases.title: Review Dependency Health Before Release
use_cases.description: Check outdated, deprecated, duplicated, and risky dependencies before shipping a release.

use_cases.title: Document License Compliance
use_cases.description: Summarize dependency licenses and flag packages that need policy or legal review.

prompt_templates.title: Scan My Dependencies
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this project dependency setup. Identify outdated, vulnerable, deprecated, and license-sensitive packages. Recommend the safest next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Safe Updates
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an update plan for this project. Separate patch, minor, and major updates. Include testing steps and rollback considerations.

prompt_templates.title: Check License Compliance
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze dependency licenses for this project. Flag unknown, restrictive, or incompatible licenses and summarize required follow-up actions.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Full Dependency Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a full dependency audit. Include vulnerability severity, fix availability, update risk, license status, duplicate versions, and prioritized recommendations.

output_examples.input: Scan this Node.js project for dependency risks.
output_examples.output: A ranked audit summary with vulnerable packages, outdated versions, fix recommendations, and commands to verify results.

output_examples.input: Check whether our Python dependencies are safe to update.
output_examples.output: An update plan grouped by patch, minor, and major changes, with test steps for each group.

output_examples.input: Prepare a dependency license report for release approval.
output_examples.output: A license compliance summary with allowed licenses, packages needing review, and unresolved license gaps.

best_practices: Run package manager audit tools before applying updates.
Update critical security fixes first and test after each change group.
Review changelogs and lock file diffs before merging dependency updates.
anti_patterns: Do not run force update commands without reviewing breaking changes.
Do not treat generated audit summaries as authoritative vulnerability data.
Do not approve unknown or restrictive licenses without policy review.

faq.question: Which package managers does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers npm, pip, Poetry, Maven, Gradle, and Cargo guidance.

faq.question: Can it automatically fix vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: It can suggest package manager commands, but fixes should be reviewed and tested before merge.

faq.question: Does it verify license compliance?
faq.answer: It helps prepare license reports and flags issues, but final approval depends on project policy.

faq.question: Does the helper script execute package manager commands?
faq.answer: No. The helper script parses dependency files and prints structured dependency information.

faq.question: Is it suitable for release readiness checks?
faq.answer: Yes. It can organize vulnerability, update, deprecation, and license findings before release.

faq.question: Can it replace Dependabot or Snyk?
faq.answer: No. It complements dedicated tools by interpreting results and planning follow-up work.
